assets.abstract: Misidentification syndromes have been described for a long time, but the major developments occurred in the 19th and 29th centuries. Many reviews have discussed the various aspects of delusional misidentification syndromes (DMSs). However, there is a lack of updated comprehensive review. This narrative review aims to review the historical aspects, classification, clinical aspects, epidemiology, etiological models, assessment, differential diagnosis, and management of DMS.
